title: Cover Flow, Anchor Menus, VF Scroll, :user-valid, base-select
description: Five cutting-edge CSS patterns: scroll-driven 3D cover flow, anchor-positioned popover menus, scroll-driven variable font weight, CSS-native form validation, and the new styleable native select.

drops.title: CSS Scroll-Driven Cover Flow
drops.description: Pure CSS 3D cover flow carousel — named view timelines rotate each card as it scrolls through the viewport.
drops.concept: Each list item declares view-timeline-name: --cover and view-timeline-axis: inline, making the browser track the element's traversal through the horizontal scroll port. The inner cover div binds animation-timeline: --cover and runs a keyframe sequence that rotateY(-45deg) as it enters, straightens to 0deg and scale(1.15) at center stage, then rotateY(45deg) as it exits. A second animation on the li peaks z-index:100 at 50% so the center card overlays neighbors. scroll-snap-type: x mandatory centers one card at a time. No JavaScript animation code whatsoever.

drops.title: Anchor-Tethered Context Menus
drops.description: CSS anchor-name tethers popover menus to their triggers — auto-flip, entry animation, zero JS for positioning.
drops.concept: Each trigger button receives anchor-name: --tN and a popovertarget attribute pointing to a menu element. The menu uses position-anchor: --tN to bind to its trigger, then position-area: block-end span-inline-start to place itself below-left. The position-try: flip-block, flip-inline rule gives the browser three viewport-safe fallback placements — previously requiring Floating UI or Popper.js. Entry and exit animations use @starting-style + transition-behavior: allow-discrete to fade and slide as display transitions from none → block.
drops.source: Chris Coyier · Frontend Masters Blog — CSS Anchor Positioning (Jan 2026)

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Font Weight
drops.description: CSS @property + animation-timeline: view() drives font-weight from 100 to 900 as each word section scrolls into view.
drops.concept: The @property --fw rule registers a custom property with syntax '<number>' so the browser can interpolate it smoothly. A @keyframes block animates --fw from 100 to 900 (and -webkit-text-stroke from visible to transparent, so the outlined ghost becomes a solid filled weight). Each word element sets animation-timeline: view() and animation-range: entry 0% cover 60%, tying the interpolation precisely to how far the element has entered the viewport. Staggered animation-range per child creates a wave. Works with system-ui which is a variable font on all modern systems.
drops.source: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations spec + MDN + Bramus Van Damme

drops.title: CSS-Native Form Validation
drops.description: :user-valid pops in a checkmark with spring easing; :user-invalid shakes the field — zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: The :user-valid and :user-invalid pseudo-classes solve the UX problem of :valid/:invalid (which fire immediately on load). They only activate after the user has interacted with a field, eliminating pre-blur red states. A checkmark sibling span transitions from scale(0) to scale(1) using cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) (spring overshoot) when the parent :has(input:user-valid). The :user-invalid state triggers a @keyframes shake animation (translateX oscillation) and a sibling error message slides in. The entire validation UI — including error messages — uses only CSS adjacent and descendant selectors.

interaction.mouse: type in fields then click elsewhere to trigger validation states

drops.title: Native Styleable <select>
drops.description: appearance: base-select (Chrome 135+) gives the native select element full CSS styling — icons, custom picker, animated open/close.
drops.concept: Setting appearance: base-select on a <select> element opts it into a new rendering mode where the browser exposes the picker container via the ::picker(select) pseudo-element and the chevron via ::picker-icon. In this mode, <option> elements can contain arbitrary HTML (icons, descriptions), and all CSS applies. The picker uses CSS anchor positioning internally so position-try handles viewport overflow. @starting-style + transition-behavior: allow-discrete animates the picker open/close. Progressive enhancement via @supports ensures fallback to native OS select in unsupported browsers.
drops.source: Una Kravets · Chrome for Developers Blog (March 2025)

interaction.mouse: click any select to open the styled picker
interaction.click: choose an option to see the selection update
